title: Tomophyllum walleri
intro: Tomophyllum walleri is a species of plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as a type of fern. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of infections. The bioactive constituents of Tomophyllum walleri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tomophyllum walleri is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may also be found in herbal capsules or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and applications.
taxonomy: Tomophyllum walleri is a species of plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the spleenwort family.
